en.text: O Lord of the senses of all beings
O Lord Kṛṣṇa, whose eyes are like the petals of a lotus flower
O most valiant of the Kauravas
O Lord with a thousand arms
O Arjuna, who can skilfully shoot arrows even with his left hand
this previously unseen universal form of Yours
giving up all attachment to material objects
pertaining to the divine glories of Your Supreme Self
with Your many arms, bellies, mouths, and eyes
like the blazing fire of the sun
in this eternal form of Mine, of human features
and also the Gandharvas, Yakṣas, Asuras, and perfected beings
and resembling the fire of the universal annihilation
placing the palms of his hands together in prayer
as the most merciful Śrī Kṛṣṇa
the great sages and perfected beings
out of Your mercy for me
nor by severe austerities such as the Chāndrāyaṇ vow
neither by study of the Vedas or Vedic sacrifices
Neither by study of the Vedas
(the mistakes) of a son, friend or beloved
thinking of You as a friend
of charming (human) feature, dressed in yellow as familiar to Arjuna
“May auspiciousness come to the world” – saying this
that which is transcendental, the Supreme
in that form of four arms
You are the manifest and the unmanifest, the material effect and cause
You are the god of air
beg You, the inconceivable, to please forgive me
and facing all directions of the universe
of the glories pertaining to Yourself
and Your eyes are the sun and moon
preserver of Vedic sanātan-dharma, the eternal religion

ru.text: этот Твой доселе невиданный вселенский образ
во время трапезы и т. д
о Господь Кр̣ш̣н̣а, чьи глаза подобны лепесткам цветка лотоса
увенчанный диадемой, дарованной Индрой после победы над демонами данавами (Арджуна)
благоприятным ритуалам и деятельности, предписанным Ведами
о Арджуна, способный даже левой рукой посылать стрелы в цель
Хари (Тот, Кто избавляет от всего неблагоприятного), Ш́рӣ Кр̣ш̣н̣а
